# services.wstunnel

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.wstunnel`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.wstunnel.clients` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | `wstunnel` clients to set up. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.addNetBind` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Whether add CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE to the tunnel service, this should be enabled if you want to bind port < 1024. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.autoStart` | `boolean` | Whether to enable starting this wstunnel instance automatically. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.connectTo` | `string` | Server address and port to connect to. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable this `wstunnel` instance. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Environment file to be passed to the systemd service. Useful for passing secrets to the service to prevent them from being world-readable in the Nix store. Note however that the secrets are passed to `wstunnel` through the command line, which makes them locally readable for all users of the system at runtime. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.package` | `package` | The wstunnel package to use. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string or list of string)` | Command line arguments to pass to `wstunnel`. Attributes of the form `argName = true;` will be translated to `--argName`, and `argName = \"value\"` to `--argName value`. |
| `services.wstunnel.clients.<name>.settings.http-headers` | `(list of string) or (attribute set of string) convertible to it` | Custom headers to send in the upgrade request |
| `services.wstunnel.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable wstunnel. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | `wstunnel` servers to set up. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.autoStart` | `boolean` | Whether to enable starting this wstunnel instance automatically. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable this `wstunnel` instance. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Environment file to be passed to the systemd service. Useful for passing secrets to the service to prevent them from being world-readable in the Nix store. Note however that the secrets are passed to `wstunnel` through the command line, which makes them locally readable for all users of the system at runtime. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.listen` | `submodule` | Address and port to listen on. Setting the port to a value below 1024 will also give the process the required `CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE` capability. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.listen.enableHTTPS` | `boolean` | Use HTTPS for the tunnel server. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.listen.host` | `string` | The hostname. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.listen.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.package` | `package` | The wstunnel package to use. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string or list of string)` | Command line arguments to pass to `wstunnel`. Attributes of the form `argName = true;` will be translated to `--argName`, and `argName = \"value\"` to `--argName value`. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.settings.restrict-to` | `list of (submodule)` | Restrictions on the connections that the server will accept. For more flexibility, and the possibility to also allow reverse tunnels, look into the `restrict-config` option that takes a path to a yaml file. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.settings.restrict-to.*.host` | `string` | The hostname. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.settings.restrict-to.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port. |
| `services.wstunnel.servers.<name>.useACMEHost` | `null or string` | Use a certificate generated by the NixOS ACME module for the given host. Note that this will not generate a new certificate - you will need to do so with `security.acme.certs`. |
